title: Waltham police searching for girl, 17, missing since July 3
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Victoria 'Tori' Amore, 17, missing from Waltham since July 3; last seen leaving her home at 11:19 p.m.
Amore reportedly got into a dark sedan; she has a severe food allergy, asthma, and did not bring necessary medication.
Waltham police seek public assistance; call 781-314-3600 with any information on her whereabouts.

title: Bicyclist, 74, killed in collision with dump truck in Dartmouth, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 74-year-old bicyclist was killed in a collision with a dump truck on Route 6 in Dartmouth, Massachusetts.
The incident took place shortly before noon, and the bicyclist was pronounced dead at the scene.
The truck driver was cooperative with police; investigations are ongoing by Massachusetts State Police and Dartmouth Police.

title: Boston man charged after allegedly assaulting Burger King employee, punching customer
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Patrick Donovan, 59, faces multiple charges for assaulting a Burger King employee and customer on June 30 in East Boston.
Donovan allegedly used racial slurs and resisted arrest at the nearby MBTA Maverick Station, authorities stated.
Donovan pleaded not guilty in East Boston Municipal Court and is set to return for a pre-trial hearing on August 7.

title: Canton man charged in killing of Cambridge DPW worker, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Rayquon Brown, 29, was charged with murder for the fatal shooting of Cambridge DPW worker Xavier Bautista on July 4.
Bautista was shot near Broadway and Norfolk Street; his body was discovered an hour later by a pedestrian.
The shooting has reignited debate over Cambridge's decision to end ShotSpotter gunshot detection system before the incident.

title: Boston man dead after skydiving accident in Orange
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 28-year-old Boston man died in a skydiving accident at Jumptown Skydiving in Orange, Massachusetts on Sunday.
The man fell approximately 30 feet due to a sudden gust of wind affecting his parachute, authorities reported.
This incident is the second fatality at Jumptown Skydiving within a year, following a midair collision last October.

title: Lindsay Clancy back in court for final hearing before murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Lindsay Clancy's attempt to include testimony from women with postpartum mental illness was denied in Plymouth Superior Court.
Jury selection for Lindsay Clancy's trial over the murder of her three children begins on July 20.
Defense attorney Kevin Reddington will pursue an insanity defense, citing Clancy's postpartum mental illness and auditory hallucinations.

title: ESPN ranks Drake Maye among NFL’s top-10 quarterbacks
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Drake Maye is ranked No. 8 on ESPN's list of NFL's top-10 quarterbacks for the season.
Maye led the league in quarterback rating, yards per completion, and completion percentage.
His performance elevated the Patriots from 4-13 in 2024 to a 14-3 record and a Super Bowl appearance.

title: Australian wins first-ever Saugus Wing Eating Championship
author: Emily Turner
bullets: James Webb from Australia won the first Saugus Wing Eating Championship, consuming 7.77 pounds of wings in 10 minutes.
Joey Chestnut finished second with 7.54 pounds, followed by Massachusetts native Geoff Esper in third place.
The event was held at The Brook in Seabrook, N.H., and Webb earned $2,500 for his victory.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Linebackers edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The New England Patriots linebackers excelled under coach Mike Vrabel in 2025 but face questions over health and depth.
Harold Landry and Robert Spillane led the team in sacks and tackles, respectively, facing a strong performance challenge this year.
Rookie Gabe Jacas is an unsigned second-round pick with potential, as the Patriots deal with injuries and depth concerns.

title: Man dead after apparent drowning in Randolph
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A man died from an apparent drowning at Great Pond in Randolph on Sunday, according to the Norfolk District Attorney's Office.
Randolph police received a 911 call about a swimmer in distress around 4 p.m.; firefighters found and transported him to a hospital.
State Police are investigating the incident; no further information is available from the DA's office as of Monday afternoon.
